findings.description: Transport type field not validated against allowed values before save. handleSave constructs McpServerEntry with whatever the type signal holds (defaults to 'local'). No runtime check that type is 'local' or 'remote'. An invalid in-memory value would pass through.
findings.evidence: dialog-edit-mcp.tsx:55-72 — handleSave reads type() signal and constructs config without checking it's valid

findings.description: Simplified add form only supports 'remote' type with hardcoded type:'remote'. No validation that URL is parseable before sending to SDK.
findings.evidence: dialog-select-mcp.tsx:130-136 — McpRemoteConfig constructed with hardcoded type:'remote'

findings.description: Empty command allowed through when type='local'. handleSave splits command() by whitespace and filters Boolean — empty string produces []. isValidMcpEntry only checks Array.isArray, not length > 0. Result: spawner attempts to execute empty command array. Also timeout of 0 (from empty string → Number('') = 0) passes validation and may cause immediate timeout.
isValidMcpEntry drops invalid entries silently. Transport type that is not 'local'/'remote' returns false — entry is filtered out with only console.warn. No user feedback, no error propagation.
findings.evidence: ipc.ts:73-82 — isValidMcpEntry returns false for invalid type; validateAndFilterMcpServers at line 101-109 silently drops

findings.description: toggle.mutationFn lacks try/catch AND onError handler. If sdk.client.mcp.disconnect/connect/authenticate throws, the error is silently swallowed. User sees no feedback, UI state drifts from server state.
findings.evidence: dialog-select-mcp.tsx:195-207 — mutationFn without error handling; no onError defined

findings.description: disconnectMutation.mutationFn lacks try/catch AND onError handler. SDK disconnect throws silently.
findings.evidence: dialog-select-mcp.tsx:222-228 — mutationFn without error handling; no onError

findings.description: handleSave does not await props.onSave(entry). onSave is typed as (McpServerEntry) => void but the actual implementation is async. The returned Promise is discarded — any synchronous throw inside onSave before it reaches its try block would be an unhandled rejection.
findings.evidence: dialog-edit-mcp.tsx:55,72 — handleSave calls props.onSave without await

findings.description: get-desktop-mcp-servers IPC handler has no try/catch. If getStore or store.get throws (corrupted config), the error propagates as an unhandled exception in the Electron main process. Differs from session-export-data which gracefully degrades with { error: message }.

createAndStore and disconnect can race on the same server name. No mutex or Effect.Semaphore guards the shared State object. createAndStore calls closeClient(s, name) then overwrites s.clients[name]. If disconnect runs between these two steps, it can close the newly-assigned client. State mutations (s.status, s.clients, s.defs) are non-atomic property assignments with no synchronization.
findings.evidence: mcp/index.ts:620-650 — createAndStore (line ~627), connect (line ~638), disconnect (line ~643) all mutate the same State references

findings.description: disconnectMutation is a separate useMutation instance from toggle. While toggle.isPending serializes one toggle at a time, disconnectMutation can run concurrently with toggle. This bypasses the single-mutation serialization guard.

watch() notification handler has TOCTOU race. Checks s.clients[name] !== client before and after awaiting tool list fetch, but assigns s.defs[name] outside the atomic window. Concurrent disconnect can write stale defs.
findings.evidence: mcp/index.ts:519-536 — double-check pattern but non-atomic assignment

findings.description: DialogSelectMcp has NO ErrorBoundary. The only ErrorBoundary in the entire app is at root level (app.tsx:171). A render crash in the dialog tears down the full app tree, not just the dialog. DialogEditMcp (rendered inside a <Show> block) has no boundary either. Both rely entirely on the distant root boundary.
findings.evidence: app.tsx:171 — single root ErrorBoundary; grep shows no ErrorBoundary in any /components/ file

findings.description: toggle mutation has no onError handler, unlike addMutation in the same file which gracefully captures errors into form state. This inconsistency means toggles fail silently while add errors are displayed.
findings.evidence: dialog-select-mcp.tsx:114 — toggle uses mutationFn but no onError; compare with line 143 addMutation.onError

findings.description: NamedError 'Failed' is defined but completely unused. No Effect.fail, Effect.catch, or yield* reference to it. Meanwhile validation errors in startAuth/authenticate/finishAuth use raw throw new Error() which bypasses Effect's typed error channel.
findings.evidence: mcp/index.ts:76 — NamedError.create('MCPFailed') defined but never referenced

findings.description: connectRemote tries StreamableHTTP first, then SSE fallback. But the fallback list is hardcoded — no mechanism to prefer or exclude a transport type via config.

FI-011.summary: Transport types 'local' and 'remote' are supported. Dialog-edit-mcp has no runtime validation of the type field; isValidMcpEntry in ipc.ts drops invalid entries silently. Add form hardcodes remote type. No URL/command format validation at dialog level.

FI-012.summary: Empty command produces empty array which passes validation. Timeout of 0 from empty string passes through. No port validation exists (timeout is the only numeric field). No command length or URL format validation at any layer.

FI-016.summary: Two async handlers lack try/catch entirely (toggle.mutationFn, disconnectMutation.mutationFn). handleSave in dialog-edit-mcp doesn't await the async onSave. IPC handlers for MCP store operations lack try/catch, unlike session-export-data which gracefully degrades.

FI-023.summary: Toggle has isPending guard but disconnectMutation is separate and bypasses it. Effect service has zero synchronization on shared State object. createAndStore/disconnect race can close the wrong client. No Effect.Semaphore, no per-server mutex.

FI-030.summary: No component-level ErrorBoundary in any MCP dialog. Single root boundary in app.tsx is the only safety net. toggle mutation lacks onError handler while addMutation has one — inconsistent pattern.

recommended_architect_scope: 1) Add transport validation schema shared between dialog and IPC. 2) Add empty-command guard in handleSave and isValidMcpEntry (check command.length > 0). 3) Wrap toggle.mutationFn and disconnectMutation.mutationFn in try/catch with onError -> toast. 4) Add Effect.Semaphore per server name to serialize connect/disconnect. 5) Add ErrorBoundary wrapping around dynamic dialog injection point in use-session-commands.tsx or inside DialogSelectMcp. 6) Add write queue or atomic swap for electron-store reserved stores. 7) Fix handleSave to await async onSave or change type signature.
review_history: Prepublication review cycle on Phase 2 MCP work: commit 9e953e7be fixed 4 blockers found during structured review (async save, store guards, validation, type dedup) — but the hardening gaps mapped here (race conditions, try/catch holes, missing error boundaries) were not captured in that review.

additional_out_of_scope_findings.description: NamedError 'Failed' defined but unused. Dead code from an earlier design.
startAuth/authenticate/finishAuth use raw throw new Error() which bypasses Effect's typed error channel. Consumers cannot catch typed errors from these functions.
